key_themes: Sasa mine life revision, $120m impairment, FY2025 loss and dividend cut
Q1 2026 production in line with expectations and full-year guidance maintained
Cost optimization, headcount reduction and productivity measures at Sasa
Positive sustainability outcomes, copper market fundamentals and Kazakhstan exploration upside
Attractive dividend yield (~8%) with analyst consensus Hold and ~25% upside to target
summary: Central Asia Metals (LSE: CAML) experienced a major negative event in March 2026 when an updated Sasa Mineral Resource and Ore Reserve statement triggered an approximately $120 million impairment, a shorter expected mine life, a swing to full-year loss and a dividend reduction, causing the share price to drop.[[1]](https://www.lse.co.uk/news/CAML/)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CAML.L/) Q1 2026 operations (released 16 April 2026) were steady despite winter weather — 2,880t copper, 4,282t zinc-in-concentrate and 6,529t lead-in-concentrate — with full-year guidance reaffirmed, aggressive cost actions at Sasa (including >11% headcount reduction) and progress on Kazakhstan exploration drilling.[[3]](https://www.tipranks.com/news/company-announcements/central-asia-metals-holds-output-guidance-as-q1-production-steady-and-exploration-drive-accelerates)[[4]](https://www.londonstockexchange.com/news-article/CAML/q1-2026-operations-update/17548997) The company also released a strong 2025 Sustainability Report and Annual Report; analysts maintain a Hold consensus with an average target near 195p (current price ~153–157p) while the stock offers a high dividend yield.[[5]](https://www.marketwatch.com/investing/stock/caml?countrycode=uk)[[6]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/LON/CAML/)
sources: 20+ recent RNS/news items (LSE, company site, Yahoo Finance, TipRanks, Proactive, Simply Wall St), Limited relevant X posts (primarily official company updates from @CamlMetals; sparse retail investor discussion), Investor chat/forums (LSE.co.uk, ADVFN) showing mixed dip-buying commentary
